must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume explicitly lists Bitbucket and Git as core tools used across 4+ years in CI/CD pipeline management.

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce Copado DevOps/Release Engineer with 6 years of IT experience, none of which is in Python development, back-end engineering, or AI model training. The JD requires a Junior Python Developer proficient in Python 3.x, Django/Flask/FastAPI, relational and NoSQL databases, Docker, Jenkins, and unit testing — the candidate demonstrates zero evidence of any of these. The only overlap is Git/Bitbucket usage, which is table-stakes and insufficient. The recruiter's explicit note about wanting Python project experience and AI model training makes this an even cleaner rejection. Do not proceed — this profile belongs in a Salesforce DevOps pipeline, not a Python developer search.
strengths: GIT & CI/CD PIPELINE EXPERIENCE: Candidate has 4+ years of hands-on Git and Bitbucket usage for CI/CD pipeline management — the only JD skill with any overlap — but this is Salesforce-specific, not Python-based.

AGILE & DELIVERY TRACK RECORD: Reduced deployment time by 30% through Copado-driven processes and holds multiple corporate awards for on-time, critical production releases — strong delivery discipline, but irrelevant to this role.

SALESFORCE AI COMPONENT EXPOSURE: Resume mentions Agentforce AI component deployments — a modern signal — but this is Salesforce-native AI orchestration, not Python-based AI/ML model training as the recruiter requires.
gaps: ZERO PYTHON OR BACK-END DEVELOPMENT EXPERIENCE: The candidate has no evidence of Python (3.x), Django, Flask, FastAPI, REST API development, or any back-end coding work. This is the entire job function — not a partial gap but a complete domain mismatch.

NO AI MODEL TRAINING EXPERIENCE: The recruiter explicitly requires someone who has trained AI models using Python. This candidate's 'AI' exposure is limited to deploying Salesforce Agentforce components — no ML frameworks, no model training, no data science tooling whatsoever.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in the resume and JD specifies no maximum — no penalty applied.
JD requires In-office presence in Bangalore; candidate is based in Puducherry, India — location mismatch.
JD requires 0 years relevant experience (fresher/entry level), so no experience gap penalty applies.
JD gender preference is 'Any' — no penalty applied.
JD specifies no college preference or exclusion — no adjustment applied.

availability_reason: Candidate is currently employed at Trimble Inc. with no end date or notice period stated on the resume.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce Copado DevOps / Release Engineer
rare_assets: Quadruple Copado certification (both SFP and Metadata formats, Fundamentals I and II) — rare depth in Copado release tooling.
Salesforce Agentforce AI component deployment experience — early adopter of Salesforce's newest AI orchestration platform.
30% deployment time reduction with quantified outcome across enterprise telecom domain — high-signal delivery credential.
self_reported_unverified: 'Reducing deployment time by 30%' — cited twice but no baseline numbers, deployment volume, or audit trail provided to validate the figure.
Certificate of Appreciation for 'Trimble Console Platform' — internal award with no external verifiability or scope detail.
Bronze Accolade Award from Eviden/Atos — described vaguely as 'exceptional contribution and involvement in organisational activities' with no deliverable context.
interview_flags: Probe whether candidate has any Python coding experience outside the Salesforce ecosystem — even hobby projects or coursework.
Ask about the MBA in HR Management (completing December 2025) — does candidate intend to pivot to HR, and is the Python Developer application a genuine career interest?
Clarify notice period given current active employment at Trimble Inc. — critical for timeline planning.
Probe 'Salesforce Platform Developer I' certification — does this indicate any back-end or Apex coding depth that might transfer to Python logic?
Ask about any personal or academic AI/ML projects — recruiter specifically wants AI model training experience, and candidate mentions none.
